2603.22799.title: Span Modeling for Idiomaticity and Figurative Language Detection with Span Contrastive Loss
2603.22799.paper_summary: The category of figurative language contains many varieties, some of which are non-compositional in nature. This type of phrase or multi-word expression (MWE) includes idioms, which represent a single meaning that does not consist of the sum of its words. For language models, this presents a unique problem due to tokenization and adjacent contextual embeddings. Many large language models have overcome this issue with large phrase vocabulary, though immediate recognition frequently fails without one- or few-shot prompting or instruction finetuning. The best results have been achieved with BERT-based or LSTM finetuning approaches. The model in this paper contains one such variety. We propose BERT- and RoBERTa-based models finetuned with a combination of slot loss and span contrastive loss (SCL) with hard negative reweighting to improve idiomaticity detection, attaining state of the art sequence accuracy performance on existing datasets. Comparative ablation studies show the effectiveness of SCL and its generalizability. The geometric mean of F1 and sequence accuracy (SA) is also proposed to assess a model's span awareness and general performance together.
2603.22799.paper_summary_zh: 隱喻語言的類別包含許多變體，其中一些在本質上是非組合性的。這種類型的短語或多詞表達（MWE）包括成語，這些成語代表一個單一的意義，並不由其單詞的總和組成。對於語言模型來說，這帶來了一個獨特的問題，因為涉及到標記化和相鄰的上下文嵌入。許多大型語言模型已經通過擁有大型短語詞彙來克服這個問題，儘管在沒有一-shot 或少-shot 提示或指令微調的情況下，立即識別經常失敗。最佳結果是通過基於 BERT 或 LSTM 的微調方法實現的。本文中的模型包含這樣的一種變體。我們提出了基於 BERT 和 RoBERTa 的模型，通過結合槽損失和範圍對比損失（SCL）進行微調，並使用困難負樣本重加權來改善成語檢測，達到在現有數據集上最先進的序列準確性性能。比較性消融研究顯示了 SCL 的有效性及其可泛化性。還提出了 F1 和序列準確性（SA）的幾何平均數，以共同評估模型的範圍意識和整體性能。
2603.22799.author: Blake Matheny et.al.
2603.22799.authors: Blake Matheny, Phuong Minh Nguyen, Minh Le Nguyen
